summary: High‑energy set with scorching Garcia leads and tight jams
blurb: The first set kicks off with a punchy Touch of Grey, Loser and a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition. The second set delivers a powerhouse run through Bertha, Estimated Prophet, Eyes Of The World and a soaring Morning Dew. Jerry’s leads are on fire while Brent adds tasty keyboard textures.
review: April 20, 1983 at the Providence Civic Center captures the Dead in top‑form during their spring ’83 tour.  A tight first set bursts open with Touch of Grey, Loser and a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that sets the tone for an inventive night.  The second set is where the magic really kicks in – Bertha rolls straight into an ecstatic Estimated Prophet, which flows into a soaring Eyes Of The World before the twin‑drum explosion leading to Space, Throwing Stones and a blistering Morning Dew that still makes listeners shiver.  Jerry Garcia’s leads are described as “shredding,” “burning” and “far‑out,” especially on Looks Like Rain and Eyes Of The World, while Phil Lesh drives the groove with loud, melodic bass lines that even “blow the roof off.”  Brent Mydland’s keyboard work adds tasteful riffs throughout the jams, and Bob Weir’s vocals are strong all night, highlighted by a spirited intro to the encore “Don’t Ease Me In.”  The Miller soundboard capture (gd83-04-20.sbd.miller.27860.sbeok.flacf) offers crisp, clear audio with only minor static glitches, making it the definitive listening source for this high‑octane performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of six recordings are strongly positive, praising Garcia’s scorching leads, the high‑energy setlist and Brent’s keyboard work; two are mixed but contain no negative criticism. The consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp Miller soundboard capture with good overall clarity and only minor static glitches; it presents the full show with high‑energy performance intact.

key_highlights: Punchy first set featuring Touch of Grey, Loser and a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Second‑set powerhouse run through Bertha, Estimated Prophet, Eyes Of The World and a soaring Morning Dew
Jerry’s shredding leads on Looks Like Rain and Eyes Of The World, plus Brent’s tasteful keyboard riffs throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers shredding, burning leads and far‑out solos – especially on Looks Like Rain, Eyes Of The World and the high‑speed fusion runs that electrify the night.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass is loud, clear and melodic, driving the groove with a powerful presence that even “blows the roof off” during Morning Dew.
band_performance.Bob: Bob provides strong vocals throughout, highlighted by an enthusiastic intro to the encore “Don’t Ease Me In.”
band_performance.Brent: Brent adds tasteful keyboard riffs and solid comping that enhance the jams, particularly on the Bertha‑Estimated Prophet transition.
